input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.reasoningText:
The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to migrate it to `.runsettings` after upgrading MSTest packages. Let me first look at their files to understand the current configuration, then provide guidance.

Let me check the relevant skill - migrate-mstest-v1v2-to-v3 seems relevant here since .testsettings migration is part of that process.

data.content: `.testsettings` files are **not supported** by modern MSTest (v3). You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's your configuration mapped over:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces <Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" /> -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Replaces <TestDirectory useLoadContext="true" /> -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the CodeCoverage DataCollector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has an MSTest v3 project with a `.testsettings` file. `.testsettings` files are not supported in modern MSTest — they need to migrate to `.runsettings`. Let me create a `.runsettings` file that preserves the three settings:

1. **Test deployment** — The `AssemblyResolution` with `useLoadContext="true"` maps to deployment settings
2. **Test timeout** — `TestTimeout` of 30000ms maps to `<TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` in MSTest section
3. **Code coverage data collector** — Maps to the `DataCollectors` section in `.runsettings`


Now I'll create the `.runsettings` file and update the project file to reference it.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces <Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" /> -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Replaces <TestDirectory useLoadContext="true" /> -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the CodeCoverage DataCollector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content: Now wire the `.runsettings` into your project so it's picked up automatically:

Here's how each setting was migrated:

| `.testsettings` | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` |
| `<TestDirectory useLoadContext="true" />` | `<MSTest><DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>` (load-in-place) |
| `CodeCoverage DataCollector (uri v1.0)` | `DataCollector` under `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` (uri v2.0) |

You can now **delete `local.testsettings`** — it is no longer read by MSTest v3. The `.runsettings` file is referenced automatically via the `<RunSettingsFilePath>` property, or you can pass it explicitly:

```
dotnet test --settings local.runsettings
```
